HOME DOCUMENTATION GITHUB

A source-based Linux distribution

ABOUT

This is the most important part - I think it’s important to decide what the target audience for Lunar will be. It’s a source distro, so it’s not for amatuers. Is it for older machines? Servers? Can programmers use it? Should it be a learning experience like LFS, or should it be your daily desktop?

APPLICATIONS

list of commonly used apps that everyone wants to know about - this again depends on the target audience.

Either we want to reassure everyone that it has all the usual programs that every Linux distro has, or we want to show things that set it apart. Do we have applications that others don’t?

DOWNLOAD

Considering the rolling release nature of Lunar, users should probably be pointed to the newest nightly, rather than a year-old beta.

Or maybe there should be monthly or quarterly releases?

Regardless, there should be some text here describing the difference between the stable and the nightly, and what the user can expect from each.

August 2020 Nightly

CONTACT

Contact info - other than IRC and Github, how to contact the project? Should there be other ways to connect? It all means extra work for someone.

A generic email, like info@lunar-linux.org? Maybe setting up a telegram page? A subreddit? A facebook page? Discord?

If the only ways to stay in touch are IRC and Github, those links will stay at the top and this section won’t be included at all.
